text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Right flank pain and gross hematuria. Palpable right flank mass on physical examination. Rule out renal neoplasm and evaluate for metastatic disease. 63-year-old female with no prior oncologic history.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed in multiple phases following administration of 120 mL Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast. Acquisition phases: non-contrast survey, corticomedullary phase (25 seconds), nephrographic phase (90 seconds), and excretory phase (7 minutes). Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformats generated and reviewed. Slice thickness: 2.5 mm axial, 3 mm coronal and sagittal reconstructions. DLP: 1,842 mGy·cm. CTDIvol: 14.8 mGy.

COMPARISON:
No prior cross-sectional imaging available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The trachea and central airways are patent without endobronchial lesion. The lungs are well-inflated bilaterally. Multiple pulmonary nodules are identified:
  - Right lower lobe: Solid pulmonary nodule in the posterior basal segment measuring 1.8 x 1.6 cm (series 4, image 142). The nodule demonstrates mild contrast enhancement and does not communicate with the bronchial tree. Morphology is smooth and rounded. This represents the dominant pulmonary metastasis and is designated as a primary target lesion.
  - Right lower lobe: Second solid pulmonary nodule in the lateral basal segment measuring 0.9 x 0.8 cm (series 4, image 158). Smooth margins. No satellite nodules.
  - Left lower lobe: Solid subpleural pulmonary nodule in the posterior basal segment measuring 0.7 x 0.6 cm (series 4, image 167). Peripheral location, smooth contour.
  - Right lower lobe: Additional subcentimeter solid nodule measuring 0.4 cm (series 4, image 151).
  - Left upper lobe: Subcentimeter solid nodule measuring 0.3 cm (series 4, image 72). This likely represents a benign granulomatous nodule but is noted.
No ground-glass opacities, consolidation, or interstitial abnormality. No endobronchial lesions. No pneumothorax.

Pleura:
No pleural effusion. No pleural thickening or pleural-based masses. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in caliber. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. Thyroid gland appears unremarkable on the superior scout images.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures:
No rib fractures. No lytic or blastic osseous lesions within the visualized thoracic skeleton. Mild multilevel thoracic spondylosis.

ABDOMEN:

Right Kidney / Primary Renal Mass:
A large, heterogeneous, predominantly solid mass arises from the upper and mid pole of the right kidney, measuring 14.2 x 11.8 x 10.3 cm (AP x transverse x CC). The mass demonstrates marked heterogeneity with areas of peripheral and internal contrast enhancement in the corticomedullary phase and central areas of low attenuation consistent with geographic necrosis. Multiple foci of coarse calcification are identified within the mass. The normal renal parenchyma of the mid and lower pole of the right kidney is compressed and displaced inferiorly; the right kidney measures 17.1 cm in greatest longitudinal dimension. There is no clear fat macronodular component to suggest angiomyolipoma. The posterior margin of the mass contacts and appears to infiltrate the perirenal fat, with stranding of the perinephric fat (Gerota's fascia appears intact though markedly stretched). No involvement of the right adrenal gland is identified on current examination, though the superior aspect of the mass abuts the lower pole of the right adrenal gland.

Renal Vein / Inferior Vena Cava:
Critically, there is macroscopic tumor thrombus extending from the right renal mass into the right renal vein. The renal vein is expanded and nearly completely filled with heterogeneous, enhancing thrombus measuring approximately 3.8 cm in length within the visualized right renal vein. The thrombus extends to the junction of the right renal vein and the inferior vena cava (IVC). There is no definitive extension of thrombus into the IVC lumen; the IVC appears patent without intraluminal filling defect. This is consistent with a Mayo Clinic venous thrombus classification Grade 2 (infradiaphragmatic, involvement of the right renal vein and renal vein-IVC junction). Coronal reformatted images confirm absence of suprarenal IVC or right atrial extension.

Left Kidney:
The left kidney is normal in size, morphology, and enhancement pattern. No renal mass, cyst, or hydronephrosis. Left renal vein patent.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes:
A right retroperitoneal lymph node at the level of the right renal hilum is enlarged, measuring 2.4 x 1.9 cm (series 5, image 88). The node demonstrates heterogeneous enhancement with a central low-attenuation area possibly reflecting early necrosis. No additional right-sided retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy above the pelvic brim. No left para-aortic or aortocaval lymphadenopathy. Normal-sized preaortic, celiac, and mesenteric lymph nodes.

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. No hepatic lesions, focal areas of decreased or increased attenuation, or hepatic masses identified on multiphase imaging. Normal intrahepatic and extrahepatic biliary ductal caliber. No intrahepatic biliary duct dilation. Portal vein patent and normal in caliber.

Gallbladder and Biliary Tree:
Gallbladder is present and unremarkable without wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid, or cholelithiasis. Common bile duct normal caliber (4 mm).

Pancreas:
The pancreas is normal in signal intensity, size, and morphology throughout head, neck, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collections.

Spleen:
Normal size (9.8 cm in greatest dimension) and homogeneous enhancement. No focal splenic lesion.

Adrenal Glands:
Right adrenal gland: The right adrenal gland is identified medial to the right renal mass. The gland is slightly displaced but no discrete adrenal mass or metastatic nodule is identified. Appearances are those of a normal right adrenal gland, though assessment is limited given the adjacent large primary tumor.
Left adrenal gland: Normal morphology and size. No adrenal mass.

Stomach and Bowel:
Stomach is unremarkable. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber, wall thickness, and mucosal pattern throughout. The colon is unremarkable without thickening, mass, or diverticulitis. Appendix is visualized and is normal. No intra-abdominal free fluid.

Vascular:
Aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. No significant atherosclerotic plaque. Superior mesenteric artery, celiac axis, and inferior mesenteric artery origins are patent. The IVC is patent without filling defect as described above.

PELVIS:

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is moderately distended with smooth wall contour. No filling defect, wall thickening, or mucosal irregularity. No bladder mass identified.

Gynecologic Structures:
Uterus is normal in size and morphology for age. No adnexal masses. No pelvic free fluid. Small scattered nabothian cysts at the cervix (incidental).

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Bilateral iliac and inguinal lymph nodes are normal in size.

Muscular and Osseous Structures:
No pelvic floor mass. No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions within the imaged pelvis, sacrum, or visualized proximal femora. Mild degenerative changes of the sacroiliac joints bilaterally.

IMPRESSION:

1. LARGE RIGHT RENAL MASS: A 14.2 x 11.8 x 10.3 cm heterogeneous, predominantly solid right renal mass with central necrosis and calcification arising from the upper and mid pole is highly suspicious for primary renal cell carcinoma. The imaging characteristics, including avid peripheral enhancement with central necrosis, are most consistent with clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The mass invades the perinephric fat (at minimum pT3a).

2. RENAL VEIN TUMOR THROMBUS: Macroscopic tumor thrombus extending into and expanding the right renal vein, reaching the right renal vein-IVC junction without definitive IVC or right atrial extension. This is consistent with at least Mayo Grade 2 venous tumor thrombus (pT3b). Direct assessment of IVC involvement should be further characterized with MRI of the abdomen if clinically indicated prior to surgical planning.

3. REGIONAL LYMPH NODE METASTASIS: Enlarged right retroperitoneal lymph node at the right renal hilum measuring 2.4 x 1.9 cm with heterogeneous enhancement, highly suspicious for nodal metastasis (N1).

4. PULMONARY METASTASES: Multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with distant metastatic disease (M1), including: dominant right lower lobe nodule (1.8 cm), right lower lobe second nodule (0.9 cm), and left lower lobe nodule (0.7 cm), plus two additional subcentimeter nodules bilaterally. The pattern and distribution are consistent with hematogenous metastatic spread.

5. NO HEPATIC, ADRENAL, OR OSSEOUS METASTASES identified.

6. NO INFERIOR VENA CAVA SUPRADIAPHRAGMATIC EXTENSION or right atrial involvement.

OVERALL STAGING IMPRESSION: Imaging findings are consistent with right renal cell carcinoma, clinical stage IV (T3b N1 M1) per AJCC 8th Edition criteria, with macroscopic renal vein thrombus, regional retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and bilateral pulmonary metastases. Dedicated brain MRI is recommended to complete metastatic staging.
